name: BIZARRE PAROSTEAL OSTEOCHONDROMATOUS PROLIFERATION
definition: NORA LESION benign exophytic surface lesion of bone

demographics.Location: small tubular bones of hands + feet; proximal and middle phalanges (92%);

distribution: small tubular bones of hands + feet; proximal and middle phalanges (92%);
discriminating_features: well-marginated ossified broad-based lesion arising from bone cortex, usually without
cortical + medullary discontinuity from underlying host bone

general: well-marginated ossified broad-based lesion arising from bone cortex, usually without
cortical + medullary discontinuity from underlying host bone

clinical.Rx: surgical excision with wide margins in symptomatic patient
clinical.Prognosis: 55% recurrence rate within 2 years of excision
